name: RAG Team
description: A multiagent team that builds end-to-end RAG solutions using weave-cli. Includes planner, data engineers, search engineers, eval engineers, and ops engineers working in parallel workflows across 10 vector databases.
author: ClawMax Team

communities.name: RAG Team
communities.description: Multiagent team building RAG solutions with weave-cli

groups.name: Planning
groups.description: Requirements gathering, architecture design, lifecycle coordination

groups.name: Data Pipeline
groups.description: Collection creation, schema design, data ingestion, backup

groups.name: Search & QA
groups.description: Query tuning, agent configuration, embedding comparison

groups.name: Quality
groups.description: Eval datasets, benchmarks, quality gates, iteration recommendations

workflows.name: RAG Setup
workflows.description: Gather requirements, choose the target RAG stack, and define the work plan
workflows.content:
# RAG Setup

You are the RAG Planner. The RAG team just came online.

## Project Configuration
> **Customize these before applying:**

- **Data sources:** [docs, tickets, wiki, product specs, code, mixed]
- **Primary user questions:** [support QA, internal search, analyst research, mixed]
- **Quality bar:** [fast prototype, balanced, production-grade]
- **Candidate vector databases:** [pgvector, Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, mixed]
- **Target output artifact:** [architecture brief, runbook, deployment plan, mixed]

## Your Tasks
1. Restate the user problem, source data, and quality bar
2. Recommend the initial vector database and retrieval strategy
3. Break the work into ingestion, search tuning, evaluation, and deployment lanes
4. Assign success criteria for each specialist lane
5. Post the execution plan and open questions to Status

workflows.name: Data Pipeline
workflows.description: Define the ingestion plan, collection structure, and validation checkpoints
workflows.content:
# Data Pipeline

1. Propose the collection layout, schema assumptions, and chunking approach
2. Identify the highest-risk ingestion sources and any cleanup needs
3. Outline the dry-run, full ingest, and backup sequence the team should execute
4. Define the validation checks for counts, freshness, and source coverage
5. Post the ingestion plan and expected outputs to Data Pipeline

workflows.name: Search Tuning
workflows.description: Propose the search strategy, query test set, and tuning experiments
workflows.content:
# Search Tuning

1. Translate the user questions into a representative query set
2. Compare the recommended search modes: semantic, keyword, and hybrid
3. Note which embedding or reranking experiments are worth trying first
4. Define the acceptance criteria for relevance, citation quality, and latency
5. Post the recommended tuning plan and tradeoffs to Search & QA

workflows.name: Eval Cycle
workflows.description: Define the evaluation rubric, benchmark set, and quality gate
workflows.content:
# Eval Cycle

1. Propose the baseline evaluation set and the metrics that matter most
2. Define the pass/fail thresholds for answer quality, citations, and coverage
3. Identify the top failure modes the evaluation should catch early
4. Recommend the first iteration loop if results land below the target bar
5. Post the evaluation framework and quality gate to Quality

workflows.name: Deploy
workflows.description: Prepare the rollout plan, health checks, and day-2 runbook
workflows.content:
# Deploy

1. Recommend the first deployment environment and runtime shape
2. List the rollout steps, validation checks, and rollback guardrails
3. Define monitoring, backup, and day-2 ownership expectations
4. Flag any prerequisites the team must clear before deployment
5. Post the deployment plan and go/no-go criteria to Ops

workflows.name: Solution Brief
workflows.description: Produce the final RAG recommendation brief and rollout plan
workflows.content:
# Solution Brief

1. Synthesize the setup, ingestion, search, evaluation, and deployment findings
2. Produce a concise recommended architecture with the chosen tradeoffs
3. Include the key risks, readiness gaps, and next execution steps
4. Create a final artifact such as `rag_solution_brief.md` or a GitHub issue plan if requested
5. Post the final brief and artifact links to Status
